setting.note: The Soviet-era Vilnius of a paranoid mind: the capital of occupied Lithuania in the 1970s, its baroque Old Town, its library and its river all watched and warped by an unseen 'Them'. The map holds to the historic centre, from the university library and Cathedral Square up the castle hill to Gediminas Avenue, then renamed for Lenin, and the bend of the Neris.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Ričardas Gavelis, first published 1989. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Vilnius Poker, Ričardas Gavelis (1989). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: A dried, bricked red for the baroque city and the menace pressing on it. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
